label: Programming model v2
description: Confirms the project uses the Azure Functions Python v2 decorator-based programming model (via @app. or @bp. decorators).
hint: Use func.FunctionApp() and decorator-based triggers such as @app.route().

label: Python version
description: Checks if the current Python version is 3.10 or higher.
hint: Use Python 3.10 or higher to match Azure Functions Doctor support.
source_title: Azure Functions Python developer guide — Supported Python versions
why_it_matters: Azure Functions only supports Python 3.10+ for v2. Running an older interpreter causes immediate deployment failures and unsupported runtime errors.
symptoms: Deployment fails with 'Python version not supported'; functions fail to start; 3.9 or earlier behaviours may cause silent type mismatches.

label: Virtual environment
description: Checks if a virtual environment is activated (venv, conda, or uv).
hint: Activate a virtual environment using: 'source venv/bin/activate'.

label: Python executable
description: Checks if the Python executable path exists.
hint: Ensure Python is installed and sys.executable points to a valid path.

label: requirements.txt
description: Checks if requirements.txt exists at the project root.
hint: Add a requirements.txt file to declare your dependencies.
source_title: Azure Functions Python developer guide — Managing dependencies
why_it_matters: Azure Functions reads requirements.txt to install packages at deployment time. Without it, the runtime cannot resolve dependencies and the function app will fail to start.
symptoms: ModuleNotFoundError at runtime; deployment pipeline fails with missing package errors; cold start crashes.

label: azure-functions package
description: Checks if the azure-functions package is declared in requirements.txt.
hint: Add azure-functions to requirements.txt.
why_it_matters: The azure-functions SDK is the binding between user code and the Functions host. Without it, triggers and bindings cannot be parsed and the app will not start.
symptoms: ImportError for azure.functions; function app fails to load; no triggers are registered.

label: azure-functions-worker not pinned
description: Warns if azure-functions-worker is directly declared in requirements.txt. The Azure Functions platform manages the worker runtime; pinning it can cause deployment failures.
hint: Remove azure-functions-worker from requirements.txt. The Azure Functions platform manages the worker runtime automatically.

label: host.json
description: Checks if host.json exists at the project root.
hint: Add host.json to configure the Azure Functions host runtime.
why_it_matters: host.json is required by the Azure Functions runtime to configure the host process. Without it the function app cannot start.
symptoms: Function app fails to start locally or in Azure; 'Missing host.json' error in logs.

label: host.json version
description: Checks that host.json declares "version": "2.0" as required by the v2 runtime.
hint: Add or correct "version": "2.0" in host.json.
why_it_matters: The runtime version field gates which host features are enabled. An incorrect or missing version causes the host to fall back to v1 behaviour or refuse to start.
symptoms: Runtime version mismatch errors; unexpected v1-style function loading; host startup failure.

label: local.settings.json
description: Checks if local.settings.json exists for local development.
hint: Create local.settings.json for local development if needed.

label: Azure Functions Core Tools (func)
description: Checks if Azure Functions Core Tools is available on PATH.
hint: Install Azure Functions Core Tools for local development and testing.

label: Azure Functions Core Tools version
description: Checks if Azure Functions Core Tools meets the minimum supported version.
hint: Install or upgrade Azure Functions Core Tools v4 for local development.

label: Durable Functions configuration
description: Checks for durableTask configuration in host.json when durable functions are present.
hint: If using Durable Functions, ensure durableTask settings are configured in host.json.

label: Application Insights configuration
description: Checks for Application Insights connection settings via environment variables or host.json.
hint: Configure Application Insights to get telemetry and diagnostics in production.

label: extensionBundle
description: Checks whether extensionBundle is configured in host.json.
hint: Use extensionBundle to ensure required extensions are available for bindings.

label: ASGI/WSGI compatibility
description: Detects whether the project exposes an ASGI or WSGI callable.
hint: Verify that any ASGI or WSGI app is exposed as expected for the Functions host.

label: Detect unused or invalid files
description: Detects common unwanted files in the project root or deployment package.
hint: Remove or exclude unwanted files from deployment to reduce package size and avoid accidental secrets.

label: .funcignore
description: Checks if a .funcignore file is present to control what gets deployed.
hint: Add a .funcignore file to exclude test files, venv, and other non-deployment assets.

label: local.settings.json not git-tracked
description: Checks that local.settings.json is not tracked by git, which could expose secrets in version control.
hint: Add local.settings.json to .gitignore to prevent accidental secret exposure in version control.

label: extensionBundle v4 recommended
description: Checks that extensionBundle in host.json uses the recommended v4 version range [4.*, 5.0.0).
hint: Update extensionBundle in host.json to use id 'Microsoft.Azure.Functions.ExtensionBundle' with version '[4.*, 5.0.0)'.
